rationale: The citing paper uses Thomadsen (2012) to support a specific prior finding: entry of a competing product can raise an incumbent's profit by alleviating price competition. It then explicitly contrasts its own result against that mechanism, arguing that in a decentralized channel setting, entry can still be profit-improving even when price competition intensifies. This gives the cited paper a clear role in framing and positioning the contribution, not just a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Thomadsen (2012)'s line of work on profit-increasing competition and extends it to manufacturer-retailer channel competition with vertical differentiation. It shows a different pathway for the positive effect, via pass-through and double-marginalization rather than softened price competition.
